rationale: X's core claim — that both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts identically, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was never semantically justified — is essentially the exact reasoning in yJader's ground-truth inline comment, grounded with specific line refs. Y covers the same mechanism but reframes it as an undertested risk needing a follow-up regression test, missing the reviewer's actual point, and its verdict 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' contradicts the two clean LGTM approvals. Y also cites a PR comment from '@akshatvishu' about HunyuanImage-3.0 that appears nowhere in the provided ground-truth thread, a likely fabrication that undercuts its 'verified/sweep' claims; X has its own smaller flaw (an internally contradictory claim that old outputs[0] would raise TypeError, while also saying it 'preserved stage_durations' from the old return). Y's suggestions are more code-concrete/actionable, but X is more accurate and better aligned with what reviewers actually flagged.
